================================================================
W4-blocking-release — expected refusal trace for project-alpha-blocked
================================================================

This is the expected refusal trace when /nacl-tl-release is run
against project-alpha-blocked with NO signed exceptions present
and NO emergency-mode env vars set.

Gates fire in workflow order. The skill emits exactly ONE terminal
headline (the first refusal it encounters). The fixture's expected
terminal headline is documented at the bottom; the per-gate
verdict table documents what each individual gate would have
produced had the prior gates not fired first.

----------------------------------------------------------------
Per-gate evaluation (independent; observed by masking prior gates)
----------------------------------------------------------------

| # | Condition                              | Verdict | Workflow detail                                  |
|---|----------------------------------------|---------|--------------------------------------------------|
| 1 | upstream-sync-unverified               | REFUSE  | upstream-sync-unverified                         |
| 2 | upstream-qa-unverified                 | REFUSE  | upstream-qa-unverified                           |
| 3 | graph-stale (live vs baseline)         | REFUSE  | graph-stale                                      |
| 4 | sa-validate-critical                   | REFUSE  | sa-validate-critical                             |
| 5 | missing-prod-golden-path               | REFUSE  | missing-prod-golden-path                         |
| 6 | skipped-pr / skipped-ci without prototype + exception | REFUSE  | direct-strategy-on-standard-project              |

All six conditions fire. The W4 acceptance check requires AT LEAST
FOUR of six; this fixture demonstrates SIX of six.

----------------------------------------------------------------
Terminal headline (no exceptions, no emergency mode)
----------------------------------------------------------------

The release skill encounters condition #6 first (Step 0 prelude
validates project_kind + git.strategy combination BEFORE entering
Step 1 candidate collection). Expected terminal headline:

  RELEASE HALTED — UNVERIFIED (direct-strategy-on-standard-project)

If condition #6 is masked (e.g. config.yaml swapped to
project_kind: prototype + signed exception for skipped-pr/ci), the
skill enters Step 1, collects no PRs, advances to Step 2 status
gate over commits-since-last-tag, and proceeds to Step 3 verify
production deployment. Condition #1 (upstream-sync-unverified)
then fires at the prelude of Step 2's status branching, yielding:

  RELEASE HALTED — UNVERIFIED (upstream-sync-unverified)

Continuing to mask #1 surfaces #2:

  RELEASE HALTED — UNVERIFIED (upstream-qa-unverified)

Continuing to mask #2 surfaces #5 (PROD_GOLDEN_PATH precedes
graph-staleness in Step 3b):

  RELEASE HALTED — UNVERIFIED (missing-prod-golden-path)

Continuing to mask #5 surfaces #3 (graph-staleness check in
Step 7 pre-flight):

  RELEASE HALTED — UNVERIFIED (graph-stale)

Continuing to mask #3 surfaces #4 (the SA-validate gate is
evaluated alongside the graph evaluation):

  RELEASE HALTED — UNVERIFIED (sa-validate-critical)

----------------------------------------------------------------
Closed-set Status (Codex contract)
----------------------------------------------------------------

In every scenario above, the closed-set Status: line is BLOCKED
(NOT FAILED — the gates are pre-conditions, not failures of the
release action itself).

----------------------------------------------------------------
With a valid signed exception for graph-stale (scenario B)
----------------------------------------------------------------

If .tl/exceptions/EXC-2026-05-22-valid-graph-stale.yaml is
present (and well-formed and unexpired), condition #3 is
suppressed. The skill still refuses with the FIRST remaining
condition (in the masking order above, that's #6 →
direct-strategy-on-standard-project). The graph-stale exception
DOES NOT bypass the other five conditions.

The release-status.json gains:

  "exceptions": [
    {
      "exception_id": "EXC-2026-05-22-valid-graph-stale",
      "affected_gates": ["graph-stale"],
      "expiry": "2026-05-23T08:35:00Z",
      "followup_task": "TECH-042-graph-refresh"
    }
  ]

----------------------------------------------------------------
With the expired signed exception (scenario C)
----------------------------------------------------------------

If .tl/exceptions/EXC-2025-12-01-expired-graph-stale.yaml is the
only exception present, the skill MUST treat it as ABSENT:

- Banner printed on stderr:
    Exception EXC-2025-12-01-expired-graph-stale is EXPIRED
    (expiry: 2025-12-31T23:59:59Z, now: 2026-05-22T10:05:00Z).
    Treating as absent.
- Workflow detail recorded: exception-expired
- Condition #3 (graph-stale) fires again as if no exception
  existed.

This is the W4 binding rule "Expired = blocker".

----------------------------------------------------------------
With NACL_EMERGENCY=1 + companion env vars (scenario D)
----------------------------------------------------------------

The skill prints one bypass banner per refusal (six total), writes
.tl/emergencies/<timestamp>-<slug>.yaml, appends to
release-status.json under "emergency", appends to
.tl/changelog.md, and advances. Terminal headline:

  RELEASE COMPLETE — emergency-bypass

Closed-set Status: PARTIALLY_VERIFIED (NEVER VERIFIED).
